title: Recommendation QA Baseline
overview: Representative recommendation-quality cases for regression review. This file defines expectations only and does not change product behavior.

cases.scenario: Small studio apartment where one room needs to function as both living and sleeping space, with only a small footprint available for furniture.
cases.expectedUserIntent: The user needs compact, multi-use, apartment-friendly pieces that do not overwhelm the room.
expectedPositiveSignals: Results emphasize compact living essentials such as small sofas, loveseat-scale seating, narrow tables, or dual-purpose items.
Recommendations avoid suggesting a full oversized furniture set that would dominate a studio layout.
Explanations mention small-space practicality, compact scale, or multi-function usefulness.
expectedFailureSignals: Large sectional sofas, oversized media units, or bulky multi-piece sets dominate the top results.
Results read like a standard living-room package without acknowledging the mixed-use studio constraint.
Explanations ignore space limitations entirely.

cases.scenario: Bedroom-focused request for a calm, comfortable setup centered on sleep and basic bedroom function.
cases.expectedUserIntent: The user wants bedroom-appropriate furniture and decor support, not general living-room recommendations.
expectedPositiveSignals: Top results include clearly bedroom-relevant categories such as bed frames, nightstands, dressers, or bedroom storage.
The mix supports rest, comfort, and bedroom use rather than entertaining or dining.
Explanations connect the recommendations to a bedroom context.
expectedFailureSignals: Living-room-only products such as coffee tables or TV stands dominate the output.
Results look category-confused or split across unrelated rooms.
Explanations use generic room language without reflecting a bedroom request.

cases.scenario: Living room request centered on finding a sofa-first setup for everyday lounging and guest seating.
cases.expectedUserIntent: The user primarily wants seating-led living-room recommendations with the sofa as the anchor product.
expectedPositiveSignals: Sofa or couch options appear prominently in the leading results.
Supporting items, if present, feel adjacent to a sofa-centered living-room setup.
Explanations reflect comfort, seating use, and living-room function.
expectedFailureSignals: Non-seating categories outrank sofas without a clear reason.
Results feel scattered across unrelated furniture types.
The output does not establish a clear anchor piece for the living room.

cases.scenario: Budget-sensitive request from a user who needs a practical room setup while staying firmly in a lower price range.
cases.expectedUserIntent: The user wants affordable options and would rather trade premium styling for value and utility.
expectedPositiveSignals: The overall result set skews toward lower-cost or value-oriented items relative to the catalog.
Recommendations do not cluster around premium or obviously expensive options.
Explanations mention affordability, budget awareness, or practical value.
expectedFailureSignals: Top results include obviously premium, designer-looking, or expensive items despite the budget signal.
The set mixes one cheap item with several high-cost suggestions that break the overall budget intent.
Explanations sound aspirational or luxury-oriented instead of cost-aware.

cases.scenario: Style-constrained request for a modern minimalist room with clean lines and reduced visual clutter.
cases.expectedUserIntent: The user wants recommendations that align with a clear style direction, not just generic furniture matches.
expectedPositiveSignals: Results show a visually coherent modern or minimalist direction.
Suggested pieces avoid heavy ornamentation, overly traditional forms, or mixed-style clutter.
Explanations reference clean lines, simplicity, or visual restraint in a plausible way.
expectedFailureSignals: Top results mix conflicting styles such as ornate traditional pieces with minimalist items.
The set feels stylistically random or inconsistent.
Explanations claim a modern minimalist fit for products that visibly do not match that style.

cases.scenario: Small-space constraint where the user explicitly needs furniture that can fit in a narrow room with limited circulation space.
cases.expectedUserIntent: The user wants space-efficient products that are plausible for tight layouts and everyday movement.
expectedPositiveSignals: Results emphasize narrow, compact, or apartment-scale pieces.
The set leaves room for movement and does not assume a large footprint.
Explanations mention fit, scale, or circulation in a believable way.
expectedFailureSignals: Bulky, deep, or extra-wide items dominate the output.
The recommendations would likely block circulation in a narrow room.
Explanations talk about spacious layouts or statement scale despite the constraint.

cases.scenario: Storage-priority case where the room feels cluttered and the user needs furniture that improves organization first.
cases.expectedUserIntent: The user values hidden or visible storage capacity and wants recommendations that help the room function better.
expectedPositiveSignals: Storage-capable categories such as dressers, shelving, storage beds, benches, or cabinets appear where appropriate.
The result set reflects organization utility rather than style alone.
Explanations plausibly mention decluttering, organization, or storage function.
expectedFailureSignals: Purely decorative items dominate while storage needs are ignored.
Results include attractive but impractical pieces with no meaningful storage value.
Explanations mention storage benefits that the products do not appear to provide.
